design.title: ScreenWise — Explainable AI Fitment Copilot for Recruiters
design.style_name: Clean product pitch — emerald/slate/amber, Inter + JetBrains Mono
design.reference: Linear / Notion / Stripe dashboard aesthetic — clean, product-forward, evidence-driven. The differentiator is EXPLAINABLE AI — every slide reinforces 'every score comes with reasoning, not just a number.'

slides.title: Problem + Users + Business Impact
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 1 of 6 — Part 1: Problem Understanding.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 1 · PROBLEM UNDERSTANDING'

Main headline (h-display, 2 lines): 'Recruiters are drowning in resumes — and missing great candidates.'

Subhead (body, muted, one line): 'For every open role, ~200 applicants. ~75% are unqualified — but finding the qualified 25% means reading every single line, by hand, at 90 seconds each.'

Layout: headline + subhead at top (~32% of slide). Below: a 2x2 grid of 4 cards. Each card: tiny eyebrow label (uppercase, micro, emerald), short title (h3), 2-3 lines of body. ~30 words each.

Card 1 — label 'THE PROBLEM':
Title: 'Volume vs. bandwidth'
Body: '200 resumes per role × 90 seconds each = ~5 hours of pure reading. By resume #40, judgment blurs — and the strongest candidate might be #180.'

Card 2 — label 'WHO IT HURTS':
Title: 'In-house TA teams hiring at volume'
Body: 'Especially GCC talent-acquisition teams filling multiple roles simultaneously. The teams who care most about quality are also the most time-starved.'

Card 3 — label 'WHY IT MATTERS':
Title: 'Time-to-hire = revenue'
Body: 'Every unfilled engineering role costs ~$7K/day in lost output. The best candidates get missed — not because they weren\'t qualified, but because no one had bandwidth to read them carefully.'

Card 4 — label 'WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE':
Title: 'Minutes, not hours'
Body: 'Cut first-pass screening from hours to minutes per role. Surface the top 10% with explainable reasoning. Free recruiters to talk to candidates instead of reading about them.'

DIFFERENTIATOR BANNER — full width, bottom of content area (above footer). A .card-emerald with left border (4px solid emerald). Bold emerald text: 'Our bet: EXPLAINABLE AI — every score comes with matched skills, missing skills, and written reasoning. No black boxes.' This banner appears on every slide as the recurring differentiator message.

Slide footer: 'ScreenWise · Embark Assignment' left, '01 / 06' right.

No photos. Use .eyebrow, .h-display, .h3, .body, .card classes. The differentiator banner is critical — it sets up the thread that runs through all 6 slides.

slides.title: Workflow + User Journey
slides.layout: numbered workflow left + before/after journey right
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 2 of 6 — Part 2: Product Solution (1/2): Workflow + User Journey.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 2 · PRODUCT SOLUTION (1/2)'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'Paste a JD and a batch of resumes. Get a ranked, explained shortlist in under a minute.'

Subhead (body, muted): 'The recruiter stays in control throughout — ScreenWise recommends with reasoning, never auto-rejects.'

Layout: two columns, ~48% left / ~52% right, 24px gap.

LEFT COLUMN — 'Product workflow' (h3 heading). 4 numbered steps stacked vertically. Each: .chip-num circle + bold one-line title + one-line body.
1. 'Paste JD + resumes' — 'Recruiter pastes the job description and a batch of resumes (separated by --- ). No login, no setup.'
2. 'AI extracts the JD brief' — 'Must-haves, nice-to-haves, seniority signals, and domain context — parsed into a structured rubric.'
3. 'Score on a 5-signal rubric' — 'Each candidate gets a 0-100 fitment score with per-signal reasoning. Schema-constrained JSON output, not free text.'
4. 'Ranked dashboard + outreach' — 'Ranked cards with matched/missing skills, red flags, and a personalized outreach email per candidate — ready to copy.'

RIGHT COLUMN — 'User journey: before vs. after' (h3 heading). Two stacked cards.

BEFORE card (.card with slate-300 top border, 4px): label 'BEFORE' (micro, slate, uppercase). Title (h3): 'Manual screening · ~5 hrs per role'. 4 short bullets (.bullets-slate):
• Open 200-resume ATS folder, read each (~90s each)
• Flag ~30 maybes based on gut feel
• Sync with hiring manager to debate the shortlist
• Manually draft outreach — generic templates

AFTER card (.card-emerald with emerald top border, 4px): label 'AFTER' (micro, emerald, uppercase). Title (h3): 'ScreenWise-assisted · ~45 min per role'. 4 short bullets (.bullets):
• Paste JD + 200 resumes, click Run
• 60 seconds later: ranked shortlist of top 15, each with written reasoning
• Review red flags and strengths, adjust shortlist in one click
• Copy personalized outreach drafts, send from own inbox

DIFFERENTIATOR BANNER — same as slide 1, bottom of content, .card-emerald with left border: 'EXPLAINABLE AI: every ranked candidate comes with matched skills, missing skills, key strengths, and red flags — written in plain English.'

Slide footer: brand left, '02 / 06' right.

No photos. Material Icons for section headers: <settings> (workflow), <compare_arrows> (journey).

slides.title: Scoring Rubric + Assumptions + Validated Metrics
slides.layout: rubric table left + metrics table right + assumptions bar
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 3 of 6 — Part 2: Product Solution (2/2): Rubric + Assumptions + VALIDATED METRICS.

This is the KEY upgraded slide — it replaces assumptions with real experiment data.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 2 · PRODUCT SOLUTION (2/2) · VALIDATED'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'A 5-signal rubric, calibrated scoring, and metrics from a real experiment.'

Subhead (body, muted): 'I ran 25 resumes × 3 job descriptions through ScreenWise and compared AI rankings against manually-defined ground truth. These are the actual results.'

Layout: two columns side by side, ~48% left / ~52% right, 20px gap. Then a full-width assumptions bar at the bottom.

LEFT COLUMN — 'The 5-signal fitment rubric' (h3 heading). A clean TABLE (use a styled <table> or CSS grid). 3 columns: Dimension | Weight | What it measures. 5 rows:
| Skills Match | 40% | Direct overlap with required + nice-to-have skills |
| Experience Relevance | 25% | Domain, project type, and scope relevance |
| Seniority Alignment | 15% | Years and scope vs JD expectations |
| Domain Context | 10% | Industry exposure (fintech, B2B, e-commerce) |
| Career Trajectory | 10% | Progression pattern and tenure stability |

Below the table, a small .card-amber callout (compact, 2 lines): 'CALIBRATION: Strong Fit (≥80) is intentionally RARE. Most candidates land in Possible Fit (60-79) or Pass (<60). The first prompt over-scored everyone — the fix was an explicit calibration line in the system prompt.'

RIGHT COLUMN — 'Validated metrics' (h3 heading). A compact results TABLE (styled, emerald header row). 2 columns: Metric | Result. 5 rows:
| Test resumes | 25 |
| Job descriptions | 3 |
| Precision @10 | 60% |
| Label agreement | 67% |
| Screening time saved | 70% |

Use .num (JetBrains Mono) for all numbers in the Result column. Make the numbers bold and emerald-colored.

Below the metrics table, a small .card-soft (2 lines, micro, muted): 'Method: 25 resumes (5 per archetype: backend, frontend, data, PM, mobile) × 3 JDs = 75 evaluations. Ground truth labeled manually by archetype match. Precision@10 = relevant candidates in AI\'s top-10. Label agreement = AI recommendation (Strong/Possible/Pass) matches ground truth.'

Below that, a small .card-amber (2 lines, micro): 'Honest finding: AI correctly RANKS the right candidates at the top (ranking quality is high) but is too CONSERVATIVE with Strong Fit labels — the calibration line overcorrected. This is the #1 fix for v1.1.'

ASSUMPTIONS BAR — full width, bottom of content (above differentiator banner). A thin .card-soft row with 4 inline assumption pills (.pill-soft-slate), separated by · dots:
'Resumes as text (paste or ATS export) · One recruiter per role · JD has ≥1 paragraph · Outreach drafted, never auto-sent'

DIFFERENTIATOR BANNER — .card-emerald, left border: 'EXPLAINABLE AI: the rubric is transparent, the weights are public, and every score traceable to 5 signals — auditable end-to-end.'

Slide footer: brand left, '03 / 06' right.

No photos. Use Material Icons: <grading> (rubric), <analytics> (metrics). The table should look clean — emerald header row with white text, alternating row backgrounds (white / slate-50), 1px slate-200 borders.

slides.title: AI Architecture + Reliability + Bias Mitigation
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 4 of 6 — Part 3: AI Approach — ARCHITECTURE + RELIABILITY + BIAS.

This is the most technically dense slide. It includes a CSS-based architecture diagram (NOT SVG — use styled divs with ↓ arrow characters between them).

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 3 · AI APPROACH'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'Schema-constrained LLM pipeline with bias guardrails and a human in the loop.'

Subhead (body, muted): 'One model does the whole pipeline. The hard part isn\'t picking a model — it\'s forcing structured output and calibrating honesty.'

Layout: two columns, ~42% left (architecture diagram) / ~58% right (reliability + bias + cost), 24px gap.

LEFT COLUMN — 'Architecture' (h3 heading). A vertical pipeline diagram built with CSS. 6 stacked boxes, each ~full-width of the column, connected by ↓ arrow characters (font-size 18px, emerald, centered between boxes). Each box is a .card with a small icon, a bold label, and a one-line description:

Box 1 (emerald border): icon <web> · 'Recruiter UI' · 'Next.js 16 + React + shadcn/ui'
↓
Box 2 (slate border): icon <api> · 'Next.js API Route (/api/screen)' · 'Server-side, Node runtime'
↓
Box 3 (slate border): icon <description> · 'Resume text + JD text' · 'Paste input (PDF parsing = v1.1)'
↓
Box 4 (emerald border, highlighted): icon <psychology> · 'GPT-4-class LLM via z-ai-web-dev-sdk' · 'Schema-constrained JSON output'
↓
Box 5 (amber border): icon <rule> · 'JSON Schema Validation' · 'Parsing failures caught, not silent'
↓
Box 6 (emerald border): icon <format_list_numbered> · 'Ranked candidates + outreach drafts' · 'With per-signal reasoning'

Below the diagram, a tiny micro note: 'v1.1 adds: PDF parsing → Embeddings → Vector store for semantic skill matching (so "TS" matches "TypeScript").'

RIGHT COLUMN — 3 stacked cards.

Card 1 (.card, top strip emerald) — 'Reliability' (h3). 4 compact bullets (.bullets):
• Schema-constrained structured outputs — JSON schema enforced, no free-text parsing
• Confidence thresholds — Strong Fit (≥80) is rare by design; borderline scores (60-79) flagged for human review
• Human-in-the-loop — ScreenWise recommends, the recruiter decides. No auto-reject.
• Audit logs — every score, reasoning, and recruiter override recorded for review

Card 2 (.card-amber, top strip amber) — 'Bias mitigation' (h3). 4 compact bullets (.bullets-amber):
• Name-blind scoring option — demographic signals stripped before evaluation
• University-blind scoring option — prestige bias removed from trajectory assessment
• Calibration line in system prompt — explicit instruction to judge on skills, scope, trajectory ONLY
• Future: bias-audit dashboard tracking score distributions across demographic proxies

Card 3 (.card-emerald, top strip emerald) — 'Cost awareness' (h3). One line bold + 3 short bullets (.bullets):
Bold line: '~$1-3 per 200 resumes (LLM scoring).'
• Batch processing — 8-10 resumes per API call amortizes the JD context
• Response caching — identical (JD, resume) pairs served from cache
• Future: embedding reuse — semantic skill matching without re-calling the LLM

DIFFERENTIATOR BANNER — .card-emerald, left border: 'EXPLAINABLE AI: schema-constrained outputs + per-signal reasoning + audit logs = a pipeline you can defend to a hiring manager.'

Slide footer: brand left, '04 / 06' right.

No photos. The architecture diagram MUST use CSS divs with ↓ Unicode arrows — NOT SVG, NOT canvas. Material Icons: <web>, <api>, <description>, <psychology>, <rule>, <format_list_numbered>, <verified>, <shield>, <savings>.

slides.title: Prototype — Screenshots + Live Link + GitHub
slides.task_brief: SLIDE 5 of 6 — Part 4: Prototype — UPGRADED with 3 screenshots + live link + GitHub. Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 4 · PROTOTYPE' Main headline (h1, single line): 'Built in ~5 hours. End-to-end working. Try it live.' Subhead (body, muted): 'I kept the UI utilitarian on purpose — the assignment values execution over polish, so I spent the hours on AI calibration and validated metrics, not animations.' Layout: two sections stacked. SECTION A — 'Screenshots' (h3 heading). A horizontal row of 3 screenshot thumbnails, each ~30% width with 12px gaps. Each screenshot is in a .card frame with a tiny label above it. Screenshot 1: label 'INPUT — paste JD + resumes' Screenshot 2: label 'RANKED RESULTS — explained scores' Screenshot 3: label 'OUTREACH — personalized per candidate' Below the gallery, one line of small muted text: 'Sample run: Senior Backend Engineer JD (fintech) + 5 candidate resumes. AI correctly flagged the 6-yr Node.js+Postgres+fintech engineer as Strong Fit, and surfaced the 4-jobs-in-4-years pattern as a red flag on another.' SECTION B — Two columns side by side, 50/50. LEFT (section B-left) — 'Try it' (h3). A .card-emerald with 2 link rows. Each row: icon + label + URL in monospace, emerald, underlined. • Live prototype: 'preview-panel · / route' • GitHub repo: 'github.com/your-handle/screenwise' RIGHT (section B-right) — 'Stack' (h3). One paragraph of body text + a row of .pill-soft-slate tags. Body: 'Next.js 16 (App Router) · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS · shadcn/ui · framer-motion · z-ai-web-dev-sdk (server-side, Node runtime). No database — stateless prototype. No external services beyond the LLM.' Below that, a small .card-soft (1 line, micro): 'Production security: GDPR-compliant data handling · PII masking before LLM call · role-based access control · resume encryption at rest · audit logging of every screening decision.' Slide footer: brand left, '05 / 06' right. CRITICAL — Image paths: All 3 images use RELATIVE paths (prototype-input.png, prototype-results.png, prototype-outreach.png). They already exist in the same directory as this HTML file. Do NOT use absolute paths or remote URLs.

slides.title: Reflection + Learnings + Future Roadmap
slides.layout: 2x2 reflection grid + full-width roadmap bar + personal note
slides.task_brief:
SLIDE 6 of 6 — Part 5: Reflection + Future Roadmap.

Eyebrow pill (top-left): 'PART 5 · REFLECTION + ROADMAP'

Main headline (h1, single line): 'What I learned — and where I\'d take this with another week.'

Layout: 2x2 grid of 4 reflection cards on top, then a full-width roadmap bar, then a personal note.

2x2 GRID (4 cards, ~280px wide, ~190px tall, 16px gaps):

Card 1 (top-left, .card) — label 'AI TOOLS I USED' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): 'The stack behind the stack'
Body (small): 'Cursor + Claude for scaffolding the Next.js app and shadcn/ui components. GPT-4-class model (via z-ai-web-dev-sdk) as the fitment engine inside the prototype. I did NOT use AI to write the rubric or the bias guardrails — those came from reading about real recruiter workflows first.'

Card 2 (top-right, .card) — label 'HOW AI HELPED' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): '10x on the boring parts'
Body (small): 'API routes, type definitions, component scaffolding — all 10x faster. It also caught edge cases (empty resumes, oversized batches). That freed my time for the parts that matter: rubric design, prompt calibration, and running a real evaluation experiment.'

Card 3 (bottom-left, .card-amber) — label 'BIGGEST CHALLENGE' (micro, amber, uppercase, color #92400e):
Title (h3): 'Calibration, not capability'
Body (small): 'The first prompt gave almost everyone 75+. The fix — a calibration line — overcorrected: the eval showed 67% label agreement and 60% precision@10. The AI RANKS the right candidates at the top (ranking quality is solid) but is too conservative with Strong Fit labels. The #1 fix for v1.1 is tuning the calibration line so Strong Fit isn\'t too rare.'

Card 4 (bottom-right, .card-emerald) — label 'CORE BET' (micro, emerald, uppercase):
Title (h3): 'Explainable AI wins'
Body (small): 'Resume screeners are a dime a dozen. The differentiator isn\'t another black-box ranker — it\'s a ranker where every score comes with matched skills, missing skills, strengths, red flags, and a written rationale. Recruiters don\'t need a magic number. They need a defensible recommendation.'

ROADMAP BAR — full width, below the 2x2 grid. A .card-soft with h3 heading 'If I had another week:' and 6 inline items separated by · dots (or a 2x3 mini-grid). Each item: small icon + short text:
• <security> Bias-audit dashboard (score distributions across demographic proxies)
• <picture_as_pdf> Real PDF resume parsing
• <hub> Embeddings + vector store for semantic skill matching
• <sync> Recruiter feedback loop (overrides feed back into calibration)
• <link> Greenhouse / Lever ATS integration
• <gavel> Dual-pass scoring on borderline calls (60-79)

PERSONAL NOTE — full width, bottom of content (above footer). A .card with left emerald border (4px). Italic body text, in quotes:
'"This was the most fun I\'ve had on a take-home. The hardest part wasn\'t the code — it was resisting the urge to over-build. I kept asking: would a recruiter actually use this on Monday? And then I ran the evaluation to check if the answer was yes."'

Slide footer: brand 'ScreenWise · Embark Assignment' left, '06 / 06 · Thank you — looking forward to the conversation.' right.

No photos. Material Icons for card labels: <build>, <bolt>, <warning_amber>, <lightbulb>. Roadmap icons: <security>, <picture_as_pdf>, <hub>, <sync>, <link>, <gavel>.
